UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the EduSpark platform, encompassing both students and instructors, storing their general profile information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for communication and as a unique identifier for external authentication.

name.description: The full name of the user.

bio.description: A short biography or description provided by the user.

profilePictureUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

role.description: The role of the user on the platform.

applicationStatus.description: The status of a teacher's application, if applicable.

subjects.description: For subject teachers, a list of subjects they teach.

experience.description: For subject teachers, a summary of their teaching experience.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a subject category used to organize and classify courses.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

description.description: A brief description of the category.

Course.title: Course
Course.description: Represents an educational course offered on the platform, containing details about its content and instructor.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Course entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the course content and objectives.

instructorId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the instructor who created this course. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Course)

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to the thumbnail image for the course.

price.description: The price of the course, if it is not free.

difficultyLevel.description: The designated difficulty level of the course (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced').

categoryIds.description: References to Category entities that this course belongs to. (Relationship: Category N:N Course)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the course was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the course was last updated.

status.description: The current status of the course (e.g., 'Draft', 'Published', 'Archived').

Lesson.title: Lesson
Lesson.description: Represents an individual lecture or content unit within a course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Lesson entity.

courseId.description: Reference to the Course this lesson belongs to. (Relationship: Course 1:N Lesson)

description.description: A brief description of the lesson content.

type.description: The type of content for the lesson (e.g., 'video', 'text').

contentUrl.description: URL to the main content resource (e.g., video file, external document).

textContent.description: The full text content of the lesson, if it is a text-based lesson.

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the lesson in seconds, primarily for video content.

order.description: The sequential order of this lesson within its course.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was last updated.

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Records a student's registration and overall progress for a specific course.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the UserProfile of the student who enrolled. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Enrollment)

courseId.description: Reference to the Course the student is enrolled in. (Relationship: Course 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: Timestamp when the student enrolled in the course.

completionDate.description: Timestamp when the student completed the course.

status.description: The current status of the enrollment (e.g., 'InProgress', 'Completed', 'Dropped').

overallProgressPercentage.description: The calculated overall progress of the student within the course, from 0 to 100.

LessonCompletion.title: LessonCompletion
LessonCompletion.description: Tracks a student's progress and completion status for individual lessons within a specific course enrollment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LessonCompletion entity.

enrollmentId.description: Reference to the Enrollment record for which this lesson completion applies. (Relationship: Enrollment 1:N LessonCompletion)

lessonId.description: Reference to the Lesson that is being tracked for completion. (Relationship: Lesson 1:N LessonCompletion)

isCompleted.description: Indicates whether the lesson has been marked as completed by the student.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the lesson was marked as completed.

lastViewedAt.description: Timestamp when the student last viewed this lesson.

watchProgressSeconds.description: The number of seconds watched for video lessons, allowing for resume functionality.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`userId`) is the Firebase Authentication UID. Authorization relies on `request.auth.uid` matching `userId` for write access. Public fields are readable by all. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'userId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user.

definition.description: An existence-based collection to grant administrative privileges. If a document exists at `/roles_admins/$(request.auth.uid)`, the user is an administrator. This collection is for authorization only; the schema reference is a conceptual link to a user, but the document itself might be empty or contain minimal role data. The 'userId' is the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the admin.

definition.description: Public collection of course categories. Readable by all, only administrators (determined by existence in `/roles_admins/{uid}`) can create, update, or delete categories. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'categoryId'.

Stores courses with 'Published' or 'Archived' status. Readable by all authenticated users. Only the 'instructorId' specified in the document can update or delete the course. The 'instructorId' field is denormalized from the creator's UserProfile for authorization independence. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'courseId'.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the published/archived course.

definition.description: Stores courses in 'Draft' status, privately owned and editable by the instructor. The 'instructorId' in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the document's 'instructorId' field for read/write access. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'courseId'. Documents in this collection must have 'status' as 'Draft'.

params.name: instructorId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the instructor.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the draft course.

definition.description: Lessons belonging to 'Published' or 'Archived' courses. Includes denormalized 'courseInstructorId' and 'courseStatus' (which should be 'Published' or 'Archived') for authorization independence. Readable by students enrolled in the parent course (checked via `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}`), writable only by the `courseInstructorId`. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'lessonId'. The 'courseId' field in the document must match the path's 'courseId'.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent published/archived course.

definition.description: Lessons belonging to draft courses, accessible only to the creating instructor. Includes denormalized 'courseInstructorId' and 'courseStatus' (which should be 'Draft') for authorization independence. The 'instructorId' in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the document's 'courseInstructorId' field for read/write. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'lessonId'. The 'courseId' field in the document must match the path's 'courseId'. The 'courseInstructorId' field in the document must match the path's 'instructorId'.

params.name: instructorId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the instructor.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the parent draft course.

definition.description: Tracks a student's enrollment in a course. The document ID (`courseId`) represents the specific course enrollment for the `studentId`. Only the `studentId` can read/write their own enrollments, and `studentId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid`. Includes denormalized `courseInstructorId` for instructors to query enrollments for their courses without a `get()` call. The 'id' field within the document should match the path's 'courseId'. The 'studentId' field in the document must match the path's 'studentId'.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the student.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the course the student is enrolled in (used as the enrollment document ID).

definition.description: Tracks a student's progress and completion for individual lessons within an enrollment. Only the `studentId` can read/write their own lesson completions. The `studentId` in the path must match `request.auth.uid` and the document's 'studentId' field for access. The 'id' field within the document must match the path's 'lessonCompletionId'. The 'enrollmentId' in the document must match the path's 'courseId'. The 'studentId' field in the document must match the path's 'studentId'.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the student.

params.name: courseId
params.description: Unique identifier for the course (the parent enrollment document ID).

params.name: lessonCompletionId
params.description: Unique identifier for the lesson completion record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for EduSpark prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization rules by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The foundational strategy is **Authorization Independence via Denormalization** to eliminate `get()` calls in security rules, ensuring atomic operations and simplifying rule logic.

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **User Profiles and Roles:** User profiles (`/userProfiles/{userId}`) are directly path-based, meaning `request.auth.uid` directly identifies the owner for write operations. Global roles, such as administrators, are managed via an existence-based collection (`/roles_admins/{userId}`). This allows rules to check `exists(/roles_admins/$(request.auth.uid))` without needing to read any data from `UserProfile` documents themselves, thus maintaining authorization independence.
2.  **Course Ownership & Status Segregation:** To handle different access patterns for course states, courses are segregated structurally. 'Published' or 'Archived' courses reside in `/courses/{courseId}`, where the `instructorId` is denormalized directly into the course document. This enables rules to verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.instructorId` without `get()` calls. 'Draft' courses are kept private within `/users/{instructorId}/draftCourses/{courseId}`, leveraging path-based ownership (`request.auth.uid == instructorId`) and also denormalizing `instructorId` within the document for redundancy and clarity. This structural segregation simplifies rules by ensuring all documents in a given collection path share a homogeneous security posture.
3.  **Lesson Content:** Lessons are also segregated based on their parent course's status. For published courses, lessons are at `/courses/{courseId}/lessons/{lessonId}`. To enable authorization independence for these lessons, each lesson document denormalizes the `courseInstructorId` and `courseStatus` from its parent course. This means rules for lessons can determine if `request.auth.uid` is the instructor or if the lesson belongs to a published course without needing to `get()` the parent course document. For draft courses, lessons are nested under `/users/{instructorId}/draftCourses/{courseId}/lessons/{lessonId}`, inherently securing them via path-based ownership.
4.  **Student Enrollments and Progress:** Enrollments are designed using path-based ownership at `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}`, where the `studentId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. Critically, the `courseId` is used as the document ID for the enrollment, allowing for efficient `exists(/users/$(request.auth.uid)/enrollments/$(courseId))` checks to verify if a student is enrolled in a particular course (e.g., when accessing lesson content). The `studentId` is also denormalized within the enrollment document. For instructors to list enrollments for their courses, `courseInstructorId` is denormalized into the `Enrollment` document, enabling `list` queries without `get()` calls on course documents. Lesson completions are similarly nested under enrollments, `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}/lessonCompletions/{lessonCompletionId}`, and denormalize `studentId` to ensure ownership checks are performed without needing to traverse up the document tree with `get()` operations.

**QAPs (Query as a Privilege) Support:**
*   **Public Course Discovery:** The `/courses/{courseId}` collection allows for public `list` queries as all documents within it are 'Published' or 'Archived' and thus publicly readable. This naturally supports course discovery.
*   **Instructor's Drafts:** The `/users/{instructorId}/draftCourses/{courseId}` collection, being user-specific, allows an instructor to `list` their own draft courses by querying the path corresponding to their `uid`.
*   **Student Enrollments:** The `/users/{studentId}/enrollments/{courseId}` collection allows a student to `list` their own enrollments efficiently. Additionally, the denormalized `courseInstructorId` field within `Enrollment` documents allows instructors to query and `list` all enrollments for their specific courses across all students without complex rule filters or `get()` calls on the parent Course documents.
*   **Categories:** The `/categories/{categoryId}` collection is publicly readable, supporting `list` operations for all users.

By implementing these structural segregations and denormalization strategies, the security rules become simpler (e.g., `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId`), more robust, and easier to debug, as each document carries its necessary authorization context.
